name: option
summary: Register a WordPress option with a default value via a Ready event listener.
description: Scaffolds a class that calls add_option() with a default value on Ready. Idempotent — re-running has no effect on existing options.
problem: You need a WordPress option to exist with a sensible default at runtime, and you don't want to scatter add_option() calls across activation hooks.
appliesWhen: You need a single named option (autoloaded by default)
You can express a sensible default value in PHP
Other code reads/writes the option via get_option/update_option directly
You don't need a typed wrapper around access yet
avoidWhen: You want a typed accessor (use phpnomad/option-provider, or phpnomad/setting for both)
The option is part of the WordPress Settings API (use phpnomad/setting which handles register_setting)
The data is a collection of records (use phpnomad/database-datastore or a CPT)
examples: Register an acme_settings option with default values
Add a feature flag option for the plugin
Set up a default config option for the integration
tradeoffs: One class plus a listener registration. The option lives at the WordPress level only; consumers must use get_option/update_option.
outputs: A {name} class implementing CanHandle that registers the option on Ready
A getListeners() entry mapping Ready to {name}
postApply: Edit defaultValue() to return the actual default

name.description: Class name for the registrar (PascalCase, no suffix)
name.aiHint: PascalCase. Convention: name after the option's purpose (AcmeSettings, FeatureFlags), not Register{Name}.

optionName.aiHint: snake_case, plugin-prefixed (acme_settings, not settings) to avoid collisions in wp_options.

initializer.description: FQCN of the initializer to register the listener in
